product_segments.name: Commercial
product_segments.source: Calculated from total revenue ($1,632.6M) minus Government revenue ($858.4M)

management_guidance.context: Raised from prior guidance; implies ~71% YoY growth.
Expects GAAP net income in each quarter.

competitive_position.market_share: No specific market share data provided.
competitive_position.moat: Strong customer stickiness due to deep integration of AI platforms (Gotham, Foundry, AIP) in government and commercial sectors.
competitive_position.threats: Stiff competition from large cloud and AI companies with broader platforms and resources.

key_facts.fact: Q1 2026 revenue was $1.63 billion, up 85% YoY.
Q1 2026 U.S. Commercial revenue grew 133% YoY to $595M.
Rule of 40 score reached 145% in Q1 2026.
key_facts.caveat: Rule of 40 is a metric combining revenue growth and profit margin.

key_facts.fact: Eleven consecutive quarters of accelerating revenue growth.

analyst_thesis.bull_case: Palantir is delivering ~70% revenue growth, ~80% EPS growth, 41% operating margin, and ~$760 million quarterly free cash flow. Commercial revenue is compounding triple-digits on the back of AIP, with large accounts scaling from single-digit to $20–$30 million.
analyst_thesis.bear_case: Valuation is high; consensus rating is Hold with average price target of $192.88 implying only 12.82% upside from $156.54. Bears highlight risk of competition and premium valuation.

key_institutions.thesis: Consensus implies limited upside; mix of 5 Buys, 10 Holds, 2 Sells.

data_quality.notes: Revenue segment data from SEC 10-Q and Stock Analysis; guidance from earnings releases and transcripts; competitive position qualitative; analyst thesis based on TipRanks and investing.com.
